title: RAF Typhoon declares mid-air emergency during training flight

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: A fighter jet declared a mid-air emergency while over Scotland, the RAF has said. The Typhoon, which had been operating out of RAF Lossiemouth in Moray, encountered a flight control issue at about 12:20. It is understood the Typhoon was on a routine training flight. RAF Lossiemouth confirmed the jet landed safely at the Moray base at 12:44 RAF Lossiemouth is currently hosting a number of F-15 and F-35 jet aircraft as part of Exercise Point Blank.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: It involves military aircraft operating over various areas of Scotland, particularly the Highlands and off the east coast. RAF pilot died from serious head injuries in paragliding quarry crash A cordon was put in place after the package was found during a routine security search at the Moray base.
